{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square burial mound |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square burial mound |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square burial mound |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square burial mound |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square burial mound |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square burial mound |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square burial mound |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square burial mound |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated aqqroximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional burial mounds in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional burial mounds. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is aqqroximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of aqqroximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square Kofun |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated aqqroximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is aqqroximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of aqqroximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square Kofun |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated aqqroximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is aqqroximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of aqqroximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}} |coordinates={{coord|35|40|21.4|N|139|20|54.7|E|scale:10000}} |type=Round or square Kofun |Size=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |BurialStructure=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |ConstructionDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |Artifacts=Haji ware |CulturalDesignation=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |Map=Japan Tokyo |Icon=Circle |LabelPosition=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)}}, {{ill|Hachioji, Tokyo|ja|八王子市}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated a11roximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachioji Station|ja|八王子駅}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|ja|多摩丘陵}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial artifacts were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The shape of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is a11roximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == Burial Structure == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}, with a total length of a11roximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial artifacts makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji ware fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == Notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} ==
