It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-period kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The type of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was Historic Site1 a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR [[:en:Hachiōji Station|Hachioji Station]] and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Interchange|1=Hachioji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892296}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]]. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the [[:en:Tama Hills|Tama Hills]], at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komiyamura|1=Komiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459705}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial burial goods were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The type of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == burial burial == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siltstone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 泥岩|1=mudstone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6642424}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Mitaka, Tokyo|Mitaka, Tokyo]]. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Ōyamachi|WD=Q11439134}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.672611|N|139.348528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=Round or square Kofun |area=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |burial=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |builtDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |burial goods=Haji pottery |Culturaldesignation1=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |map=Japan Tokyo |type=Circle |map_dot_label=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanicho, Hachioji|1=Ōyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439134}}, [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachioji, Tokyo]]. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-period kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The type of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was Historic Site1 a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR [[:en:Hachiōji Station|Hachioji Station]] and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Interchange|1=Hachioji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892296}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]]. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the [[:en:Tama Hills|Tama Hills]], at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komiyamura|1=Komiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459705}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial burial goods were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The type of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == burial burial == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siltstone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 泥岩|1=mudstone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6642424}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Mitaka, Tokyo|Mitaka, Tokyo]]. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11401352]] Wikidata name: Kitaōya Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Ōyamachi|WD=Q11439134}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.672611|N|139.348528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=Round or square Kofun |area=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |burial=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |builtDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |burial goods=Haji pottery |Culturaldesignation1=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |map=Japan Tokyo |type=Circle |map_dot_label=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanicho, Hachioji|1=Ōyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439134}}, [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachioji, Tokyo]]. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-period kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The type of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was Historic Site1 a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR [[:en:Hachiōji Station|Hachioji Station]] and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Interchange|1=Hachioji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892296}} on the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]]. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the [[:en:Tama Hills|Tama Hills]], at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komiyamura|1=Komiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459705}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial burial goods were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The type of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == burial burial == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siltstone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 泥岩|1=mudstone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6642424}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Mitaka, Tokyo|Mitaka, Tokyo]]. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == burial burial == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siltstone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 泥岩|1=mudstone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6642424}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|Mitaka, Tokyo|WD=Q242885|lt=Mitaka, Tokyo}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Ōyamachi|WD=Q11439134}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.672611|N|139.348528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=Round or square Kofun |area=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |burial=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |builtDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |burial goods=Haji pottery |Culturaldesignation1=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |map=Japan Tokyo |type=Circle |map_dot_label=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanicho, Hachioji|1=Ōyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439134}}, {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachioji, Tokyo}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-period kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The type of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was Historic Site1 a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachiōji Station|en|Hachiōji Station|WD=Q800868|lt=Hachioji Station}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Interchange|1=Hachioji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892296}} on the {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|WD=Q1091276|lt=Chuo Expressway}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|en|Tama Hills|WD=Q1502721|lt=Tama Hills}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komiyamura|1=Komiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459705}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial burial goods were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The type of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == burial burial == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siltstone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 泥岩|1=mudstone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6642424}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|Mitaka, Tokyo|WD=Q242885|lt=Mitaka, Tokyo}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuchū City|WD=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|Mitaka, Tokyo|WD=Q242885|lt=Mitaka, Tokyo}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Ōyamachi|WD=Q11439134|1=Ōyamachi}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.672611|N|139.348528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=Round or square Kofun |area=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |burial=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |builtDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |burial goods=Haji pottery |Culturaldesignation1=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |map=Japan Tokyo |type=Circle |map_dot_label=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanicho, Hachioji|1=Ōyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439134}}, {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachioji, Tokyo|1=Hachiōji}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-period kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The type of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was Historic Site1 a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachiōji Station|en|Hachiōji Station|WD=Q800868|lt=Hachioji Station|1=Hachiōji Station}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Interchange|1=Hachioji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10892296}} on the {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|WD=Q1091276|lt=Chuo Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|en|Tama Hills|WD=Q1502721|lt=Tama Hills|1=Tama Hills}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komiyamura|1=Komiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459705}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial burial goods were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The type of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == burial burial == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siltstone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 泥岩|1=Mudstone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6642424}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|1=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=府中市|WD=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|Mitaka, Tokyo|WD=Q242885|lt=Mitaka, Tokyo|1=Mitaka, Tokyo}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|1=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|1=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=府中市|WD=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|Mitaka, Tokyo|WD=Q242885|lt=Mitaka, Tokyo|1=Mitaka, Tokyo}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|1=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[ja:北大谷古墳]] [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Kitaotani Kofun |location={{ill|Ōyamachi|qid=Q11439134|1=Ōyamachi}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.672611|N|139.348528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=Round or square Kofun |area=Diameter or side length of 39 meters; height 2.1 meters |burial=Multi-chamber horizontal stone chamber |builtDate=Early 7th century |Occupant= |burial goods=Haji pottery |Culturaldesignation1=Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site |map=Japan Tokyo |type=Circle |map_dot_label=bottom |image=[[File:Kitaotani-kohun1.JPG|250px]]
Mound of Kitaotani Kofun }} '''Kitaotani Kofun''' (北大谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Otanicho, Hachioji|ja|大谷町 (八王子市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otanicho, Hachioji|1=Ōyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11439134}}, {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|qid=Q208863|lt=Hachioji, Tokyo|1=Hachiōji}}. It is believed to have been built in the early 7th century and is classified as a {{ill|terminal-period kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-period kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The type of the mound remains uncertain, with both round and square mound theories proposed. In March 1936, it was Historic Site1 a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site. == location == Kitaotani Kofun is situated approximately 2 kilometers north-northeast of JR {{ill|Hachiōji Station|en|Hachiōji Station|qid=Q800868|lt=Hachioji Station|1=Hachiōji Station}} and about 440 meters south of the {{ill|Hachioji Interchange|ja|八王子インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Interchange|1=Hachioji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10892296}} on the {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|qid=Q1091276|lt=Chuo Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}}. It lies on a gentle southern slope of the {{ill|Tama Hills|en|Tama Hills|qid=Q1502721|lt=Tama Hills|1=Tama Hills}}, at an altitude of about 140 meters. There are no other kofun discovered near Kitaotani Kofun. A radar survey conducted in 1993 confirmed the absence of additional Kofuns in the surrounding area. While other kofun are present in the region, they are relatively few in number.Tama Area Kofun Survey Report, p. 106. == Excavations == The first excavation of Kitaotani Kofun was conducted in 1899 by residents of the local {{ill|Komiyamura|ja|小宮村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komiyamura|1=Komiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11459705}}. This investigation revealed a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. At the time, the chamber had already been looted, and no burial burial goods were discovered. However, the remnants of the chamber provide valuable insights into its original construction. In 1933, another survey concluded that Kitaotani Kofun was a circular mound with a diameter of 29 meters. It also determined that the mound had collapsed significantly, especially toward the south. The lack of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} or paving stones indicated that these features were not originally present. A comprehensive survey, including ground-penetrating radar analysis, was conducted between September and October 1993 to investigate the entire site and identify any surrounding features, such as moats or additional Kofuns. == Mound == [[File:Kitaotani-kohun2.JPG|thumb|250px|Kitaotani Kofun mound, viewed from the southwest]] The type of the mound remains uncertain, with theories suggesting either a circular or square configuration. If circular, the diameter is approximately 39 meters; if square, each side measures about 39 meters. A surrounding moat has been identified on the west, north, and east sides of the mound, creating a semi-circle. The moat measures 3.2–4.2 meters in width and 0.5–0.9 meters in depth. No moat exists on the southern side, suggesting it was omitted from the original construction. The southern-facing slope provided natural separation from the surrounding terrain. The mound stands 2.1 meters tall. No haniwa or paving stones have been found, and it is presumed they were never part of the original structure. Fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were discovered near the mound, and their estimated production date aligns with the early 7th century, corroborating the construction period inferred from the stone chamber. == burial burial == The burial structure comprises three sections: a {{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|lt=chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen-shitsu|qq=}}, a fore-chamber, and an {{ill|Endō|lt=entrance passage|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}, with a total length of approximately 10 meters. The chamber is barrel-shaped, constructed using soft {{ill|siltstone|ja|シルト岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/siltstone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 泥岩|1=Mudstone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6642424}} with corners shaped to fit adjacent stones. Round gravel remnants suggest the chamber floor was once fully paved with stones. These features resemble other terminal-period kofun in the Tama region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|qid=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|1=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun}} in {{ill|Fuchu, Tokyo|ja|府中市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchu, Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=府中市|qid=Q226480}} and {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|Mitaka, Tokyo|qid=Q242885|lt=Mitaka, Tokyo|1=Mitaka, Tokyo}}. == Characteristics == The burial structure of Kitaotani Kofun is notable for its multi-chambered design, surpassing other kofun in the area in size and complexity. The lack of burial burial goods makes it challenging to precisely determine its context, but its construction date is widely believed to be in the early 7th century, based on the Haji pottery fragments and structural design. While it shares many similarities with other prominent kofun, such as Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun, unique aspects of Kitaotani Kofun, including its mound construction methods, suggest distinct local influences. == References == * Tama Area Kofun Survey Report. Tama Area Kofun Research Group, 1995. * ''Considering Tokyo’s Kofun.'' {{ill|Shinagawa Historical Museum|ja|品川区立品川歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Historical Museum|1=Shinagawa Historical Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11418382}}. Yuzankaku, 2006. * ''Musashi and Sagami Kofun.'' Quarterly Archaeology Special Edition 15, Yuzankaku, 2007. * ''Late Kofun Groups in Kanto.'' Rokusanshobo, 2007. == notes == == Related Articles == * {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|qid=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|1=Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun}} * {{ill|Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|北大谷古墳|version=97909566|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北大谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11401352}} [[Category:Historic sites designated by Tokyo Metropolitan Government]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
